question: Compare the description of the NuScale SG in Ch.01's general design with the detailed performance specifications in Ch.05.

nuscale_ch01_0776.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.1 Evaluation of ECCS Performance
nuscale_ch01_0776.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCAs) and related radiological consequences. It notes that while NuScale’s design generally aligns with applicable guidance, certain regulatory requirements (such as portions of 10 CFR 50 Appendix K) are exempted where technically justified, and radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by more current guidance in SRP Section 15.0.3. Additionally, some criteria are not applicable to NuScale’s design, particularly those specific to BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0452.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.1 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Auxiliary Feedwater Supply
nuscale_ch01_0452.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4, and therefore the associated water hammer guidelines are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently avoiding the water hammer concerns targeted by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0777.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.2 Radiological Consequences of Most Severe LOCA
nuscale_ch01_0777.summary: This section discusses NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA), specifically the radiological consequences of the most severe LOCA. It states that NuScale's analysis conforms to the applicable review standards, with SRP Section 15.0.3 superseding the radiological analysis criteria in DSRS 15.6.5 for design certification applications.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low, supporting a 40% through-wall defect plugging criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0451.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsPSGD B.1 thru B.4Preheat Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0451.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use preheat steam generator designs, so the regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in such systems are not applicable. The NuScale design differs from conventional large PWRs and does not require the specific measures outlined in BTP 10-2, Rev 4 for preheat steam generators.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during all operational conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0835.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.J Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0835.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 17.5, Rev 1 quality assurance criteria for design certification, early site permit, and new COL applicants. Most criteria are met or not applicable, but for document control and inspection, only partial conformance is achieved because site-specific and operational responsibilities are deferred to the COL applicant referencing the certified design. Table 1.9-3 documents these conformance details with NUREG-0800.

nuscale_ch01_0057.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - NuFuel HTP2™ Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0057.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The testing aimed to gather CHF data to develop and validate NuScale's CHF correlations, as well as to measure bundle subchannel exit temperatures and pressure drop characteristics under various hydraulic conditions. The tests were performed using a 5x5 electrically-heated simulated fuel bundle at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility in Germany.

nuscale_ch01_0842.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.Q Quality Assurance Records
nuscale_ch01_0842.summary: This section confirms that NuScale’s Quality Assurance Program Description for Design Certification, Early Site Permit, and new Combined License (COL) applicants fully conforms to SRP 17.5, Rev 1 requirements regarding Quality Assurance Records. No exceptions or deviations are noted, indicating complete compliance with the regulatory criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It also outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation between primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The electrically-heated steam generator test at the SIET facility in Italy used three instrumented tubes with controlled boundary conditions to simulate different heat flux regions (subcooled, boiling, and superheat) and focused on secondary-side performance. The testing provided data for evaluating pressure fluctuations, sizing inlet flow restrictors, benchmarking design codes, and defining steam outlet conditions, and was inspected by the NRC according to relevant procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0443.title: DSRS 10.4.7, Rev 0: Condensate and Feedwater SystemII.4 Heat Removal Capability (GDC 44)
nuscale_ch01_0443.summary: The section explains that the NuScale Condensate and Feedwater System (CFWS) does not serve as a heat removal system to an ultimate heat sink, making certain regulatory criteria (GDC 44, 45, 46) not applicable. It also notes that while some guidance for large LWRs is not relevant, the system conforms to requirements regarding sharing of components and flow-accelerated corrosion, and that NuScale does not use a steam generator blowdown or auxiliary feedwater system.

nuscale_ch01_0701.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.4 Analytical ParametersMaximize Heat Transfer from RCS to Containment and Reactor Pool
nuscale_ch01_0701.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters such as maximizing heat transfer from the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) to the containment and reactor pool. The document states that NuScale’s analysis conforms to the requirements, with no exceptions noted for this parameter.

nuscale_ch01_0290.title: SRP BTP 8-9, Rev. 0: Open Phase Conditions in Electric Power SystemB.3 Criteria for evaluating open phase conditions for passive plant designs
nuscale_ch01_0290.summary: The section discusses the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding open phase conditions in electric power systems, specifically for passive plant designs. It notes that the criteria in SRP BTP 8-9, Rev. 0 for passive plant designs are only partially met, as passive designs do not rely on AC power for safety-related functions, making some requirements not applicable. The report also confirms full compliance with various criteria for fuel storage and handling.

nuscale_ch01_0133.title: 8.39 Release of Patients Administered Radioactive Materials
nuscale_ch01_0133.summary: Section 8.39 states that the guidance on the release of patients administered radioactive materials is not applicable to the NuScale facility, as it only applies to facilities that use radio-pharmaceuticals. Therefore, NuScale does not need to conform to this regulatory criterion.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and constructed according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch01_0289.title: SRP BTP 8-9, Rev. 0: Open Phase Conditions in Electric Power SystemB.2 Criteria for evaluating open phase conditions for active plant designs
nuscale_ch01_0289.summary: The section states that the criteria for evaluating open phase conditions for active plant designs (SRP BTP 8-9, Rev. 0, Section B.2) are not applicable to passive plant designs like NuScale. Therefore, these regulatory requirements do not need to be addressed for the NuScale design.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale steam generator's design and its conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B specifies detailed design guidelines and performance requirements for steam generator systems, thus Section B provides the technical specifications that elaborate on the general descriptions found in Section A.

Section A provides a general evaluation of ECCS performance and references the applicability of technical specifications for coolant iodine activity, while Section B details the radiological consequences of the most severe LOCA using the same technical specification guidance, thus Section B specifies the detailed performance outcomes that Section A generally describes.

Section A references the general regulatory requirements and partial conformance for steam generator-related safety and iodine activity, while Section B provides the detailed design and performance specifications of the NuScale steam generators that are intended to meet those requirements.

Section A references the general regulatory requirements and partial conformance for steam generator (SG) performance and coolant activity, while Section B provides detailed design and performance specifications for the NuScale SG, thereby specifying how the general requirements in Section A are implemented.

Section A provides a general regulatory evaluation of the NuScale steam generator's (SG) conformance with safety and radiological requirements, while Section B specifies detailed design guidelines and performance criteria for the SG, thus Section B provides the technical specifications that elaborate on the general design described in Section A.

Section A provides the general design approach and regulatory conformance for the NuScale steam generator and its auxiliary feedwater system, while Section B details the specific performance and radiological requirements that the steam generator must meet during postulated accident scenarios, thus connecting the general design intent in Ch.01 to the detailed performance specifications in Ch.05.

Section A provides the general design guidelines and regulatory context for the NuScale steam generator and its auxiliary systems as described in Ch.01, while Section B presents the detailed performance specifications and component descriptions for the steam generator in Ch.05, with the information in Section B elaborating on and specifying the general design concepts introduced in Section A.

Section A provides the general design approach and regulatory conformance for the NuScale steam generator (SG), while Section B offers detailed performance specifications and operational experience for the SG tubing, thus Section B specifies the technical details that support and elaborate on the general design described in Section A.

Section A references the general design and regulatory conformance of the NuScale steam generator (SG) as described in Chapter 1, while Section B provides the detailed component and subsystem design specifications for the SG in Chapter 5, thus Section B specifies the technical details underlying the general description in Section A.

Section A provides a general regulatory conformance statement for the NuScale steam generator (SG) design, while Section B specifies detailed design guidelines and performance criteria for the SG, thus Section B provides the technical specifications that elaborate on the general design described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system and its reliance on natural circulation, while Section B details specific regulatory guidelines and performance specifications (such as those for auxiliary feedwater systems) and clarifies their applicability or non-applicability to the NuScale design, thereby specifying how the general design in Section A is addressed or exempted in the detailed requirements of Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system design, while Section B details the specific regulatory performance requirements and conformance criteria that the system must meet, connecting the general design to its required operational specifications.

Section A provides the general quality assurance and design control framework for the NuScale SG as described in Ch.01, while Section B details the specific performance and safety evaluation criteria for the SG in Ch.05, with the general design and QA requirements in A serving as the foundation for the detailed specifications in B.

Section A provides the general quality assurance and design control framework for the NuScale SG as described in Ch.01, while Section B offers detailed performance and design guidelines (such as avoiding water hammers) that must be met by the SG as specified in Ch.05, thus connecting general design intent with specific technical requirements.

Section A provides detailed critical heat flux testing data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are used to support and validate the performance specifications and regulatory conformance described in Section B regarding safety analysis and technical specifications.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system design, while Section B offers detailed performance testing data (critical heat flux) that supports and specifies the technical adequacy of the design described in Section A.

Section A outlines general quality assurance program elements such as procurement and documentation control, while Section B provides additional quality assurance elements like test control and records, together forming a comprehensive QA framework for the NuScale SG design and performance specifications.

Section A provides specific test data and correlation development for the NuScale fuel design, which are detailed performance specifications that fulfill the general design and quality assurance requirements outlined in Section B.

Section A provides critical heat flux data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary inputs for the detailed steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance testing described in Section B, as both sections address heat transfer limits and performance under operational conditions.

Section A provides the general quality assurance framework and regulatory conformance for the NuScale design described in Chapter 1, while Section B offers the detailed performance specifications and component descriptions for the steam generator (SG) in Chapter 5, with the quality assurance requirements from Section A underpinning the detailed design and performance claims in Section B.

Section A provides the general quality assurance and test control framework for the NuScale SG as described in Ch.01, while Section B presents the detailed performance testing specifications and results for the SG as elaborated in Ch.05, with the former establishing the requirements that the latter's testing must satisfy.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale steam generator's integration and function within the reactor coolant system, while Section B details the specific thermal-hydraulic performance testing conducted to verify the steam generator's operational characteristics, thus Section B specifies the technical details that support the general description in Section A.

Section A provides general design conformance and limitations for the NuScale condensate and feedwater system, while Section B details specific performance and technical specification requirements for accident scenarios, with both sections connected through their focus on regulatory compliance for system safety and performance under design-basis events.

Section A discusses the general design conformance of the NuScale condensate and feedwater system with regulatory criteria, specifically noting partial applicability of fluid instability guidance, while Section B provides detailed design guidelines for avoiding water hammer in steam generators, clarifying which guidelines are applicable or not to NuScale's unique design; thus, Section B specifies detailed performance expectations that elaborate on the general design considerations outlined in Section A.

Section A outlines general design conformance and limitations regarding heat removal and fluid instability protection for the NuScale SG, while Section B provides detailed performance specifications and partial conformance for radiological consequences and technical specifications, with both sections connected by their focus on how the SG's design and performance meet regulatory requirements.

Section A provides general analytical parameters for maximizing heat transfer in the NuScale SG design, while Section B details specific performance and technical specification requirements (such as coolant iodine activity) that the SG must meet, connecting the general design intent in Ch.01 to the detailed performance criteria in Ch.05.

Section A provides analytical parameters and performance expectations for the NuScale steam generator (SG) in accident scenarios, while Section B details specific design guidelines and performance specifications for the SG's auxiliary feedwater supply, thus Section B provides detailed specifications that elaborate on the general design described in Section A.

Section A provides analytical parameters for maximizing heat transfer in accident scenarios, which are design and operational details that underpin the radiological consequence assessments and technical specification requirements discussed in Section B.

Section A provides general design criteria and conformance statements for the NuScale SG, while Section B details the specific analytical parameters and performance specifications (such as maximizing heat transfer) that implement and demonstrate compliance with those general design requirements.

Section A provides general design criteria and regulatory conformance for the NuScale SG, while Section B details the specific performance specifications and technical requirements that the SG must meet, connecting the general design to its detailed implementation.

Section A provides the general design context and regulatory conformance for the NuScale SG's electrical independence from AC power, while Section B specifies detailed performance guidelines for steam generator operation and auxiliary feedwater systems, connecting them through the design's reliance on passive safety systems instead of traditional electrically powered systems.

Section A provides the general design criteria and regulatory conformance for the NuScale SG in terms of electrical system design and power source requirements, while Section B details the specific performance specifications and radiological consequences for the SG under postulated accident conditions, thus Section B provides detailed technical implementation and performance outcomes that specify and operationalize the general design described in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory guidance that is not applicable to the NuScale facility, while Section B addresses partial conformance with technical specifications for coolant system iodine activity, showing that both sections relate to the applicability and implementation of regulatory requirements for radiological safety, but for different aspects and with different applicability.

Section A provides the general design and configuration of the NuScale steam generators as part of the reactor vessel, while Section B details the regulatory performance specifications and conformance criteria that these steam generators must meet, connecting the design to the regulatory requirements.

Section A provides the general design context and physical integration of the steam generators within the NuScale Power Module, while Section B details the regulatory performance specifications and conformance criteria (such as iodine activity limits) that the steam generators must meet, thus connecting the design description to the regulatory requirements.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale plant's major structures and equipment, including references to where detailed system descriptions can be found, while Section B presents detailed regulatory conformance and performance specifications for specific safety systems such as the ECCS and coolant activity limits, thus Section B provides the detailed performance specifications that elaborate on the general design described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale plant's major structures and equipment, including the steam generator (SG), while Section B details specific regulatory design guidelines and performance criteria for the SG's auxiliary feedwater supply, thus Section B specifies detailed requirements that elaborate on the general descriptions in Section A.

Section A provides the general design context and regulatory conformance for the NuScale SG's electrical system, while Section B specifies detailed performance guidelines and how the NuScale design fulfills or differs from traditional requirements, connecting them through the application (or non-applicability) of regulatory criteria to the NuScale SG.

Section A provides general design criteria and regulatory conformance for the NuScale SG's electrical system, while Section B details the performance specifications and regulatory conformance for the SG's radiological consequences during postulated accidents, with both sections connected through their focus on how the SG design meets specific regulatory requirements.

Section A discusses criteria for evaluating open phase conditions in electric power systems for passive plant designs, while Section B addresses the release of patients administered radioactive materials, with both sections stating that the referenced regulatory guidance is not applicable to the NuScale design due to the plant's specific features and operational context.

Section A provides criteria for evaluating open phase conditions specifically for passive plant designs, while Section B provides the corresponding criteria for active plant designs, thus both sections specify detailed requirements for different plant types under the same regulatory topic.

Section A and Section B both document regulatory criteria that are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design, with each section specifying different regulatory requirements and justifying their non-applicability based on the plant's operational characteristics.

Section A provides detailed specifications of the reactor vessel and its components, including the steam generators, which are generally described in Section B's overview of the plant's major structures and equipment.

Section A provides a detailed description of the NuScale reactor vessel and its components, including the steam generators, while Section B outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance for electrical system design, which indirectly affects the operational requirements and safety functions of components like the steam generators described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's major structures and equipment, while Section B details the conformance of the electrical system design with specific regulatory criteria, with both sections connected through their descriptions of plant systems but focusing on different aspects (physical arrangement vs. regulatory compliance).
